title: Join request approval flow
summary: Request access → owner approves → user receives magic login link. Coexists with existing invite-code signup.
goal: Add request access with owner approval alongside signup codes for instant onboarding when desired.

indexes.name: idx_join_requests_email_pending_unique
indexes.definition: UNIQUE partial index on (email) WHERE status = 'pending'
indexes.notes: D1/SQLite supports partial indexes; prevents concurrent double-pending for same email at DB level. App still checks account exists before insert.

duplicateAndLifecycle.pendingUniqueness: Enforced at DB via partial unique index on email WHERE status = 'pending'.
duplicateAndLifecycle.accountExists: App rejects new request if accounts row exists (no duplicate signup).
duplicateAndLifecycle.rejectedThenRetry: After reject, same email may submit again: duplicate checks MUST only consider status = 'pending' (and account existence), not all historical rows. A prior rejected (or approved) row does not block a new pending insert.

phases.name: Public API (unauthenticated)

behavior: Validate email; KV rate limit (reuse checkRateLimit pattern from auth)
If account already exists → 400/409 with message to use login link
If pending request for email → friendly already-submitted response (query WHERE status = 'pending' only)
If prior rejected/approved only → allow new pending row (subject to partial unique index)
Insert pending row; handle UNIQUE constraint conflict as 'already pending' race

phases.middleware: Extend sessionMiddleware to skip this path like /api/auth

abuseAndBots.notes: Unauthenticated POST creates DB rows; rate limiting alone is insufficient against scripted floods.
recommendations: Honeypot field (hidden input must be empty; reject if filled)
Optional: minimum time since page load (client timestamp + server slack) to block instant POST scripts
abuseAndBots.csrf: Classic CSRF is lower risk for JSON POST without cookie session, but bots are the main threat; honeypot + rate limit + tight validation address that.

phases.name: Shared signup logic
phases.notes: Same behavior as invite: accounts + teams + team_members without consuming signup_codes

notes: Invalidate unused magic links for email, insert new magic link
Magic link expiry: use 2 hours (7200s) for approve flow — 15 minutes is too tight for batched approvals and delayed inbox checks; align magic_links.expires_at and email copy
If auth login keeps 15 min, either parameterize expiry per insert or use 2h globally for all magic links (product decision)

endpoints.notes: Optional reject_reason column in v1 or later

v1 includes resend (not optional): if email fails or user needs a fresh link, owner or user flow can trigger a new magic link without re-approving. Same invalidation + insert + send pattern as approve.

phases.name: Email
items: Reuse sendMagicLink for approved users; pass/site copy reflects 2h expiry if adopted
Optional sendJoinRequestNotification to OWNER_ALERT_EMAIL or first owner — v1 can be admin-UI only

files.changes: Request access toggle: email + optional message + honeypot; POST /api/join-requests; success messaging; optional client timestamp for timing check
Join requests section: table, Approve / Reject, Resend link, refresh

phases.name: Hardening and ops
items: Cron: optional cleanup of old non-pending rows
Rate limit + max message length + honeypot
Short doc in SETUP.md or docs

implementationOrder: Magic link TTL policy (2h approve path or global) + sendMagicLink/email copy
Optional owner notification email + cron cleanup

decisions.recommendation: Block or same success message for privacy; DB partial unique index backs this under concurrency
Allowed: only pending rows participate in uniqueness and 'already submitted' checks
2 hours for post-approve links (or global bump); resend-link endpoint in v1

decisions.notes: v1: new default team per user (matches invite). Join existing team is a separate future feature (e.g. team_join_requests)

unchanged.signup_codes: v1 keeps parallel instant path via codes
